skill.name: linkedin-content
skill.description: Create LinkedIn posts using hook formulas, mobile-friendly formatting, content pillars, CTA patterns, and engagement guidance.
skill.summary: Draft structured LinkedIn content for professional audiences and B2B thought leadership.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell, network, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no hidden execution code or prompt injection, but line 14 recommends a curl-to-shell installer and several examples route content through external services. The weak crypto and reconnaissance alerts appear to be false positives in Markdown text.

medium_findings.title: Pipe-to-Shell Installer Instruction
medium_findings.description: Line 14 tells users to download an installer with curl and pipe it directly to sh. This is a real supply-chain risk because users execute remote code before inspection, although the surrounding text presents it as a normal CLI installation step rather than hidden malware.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact curl-to-sh command is present on line 14, which is a well-known risky installation pattern. Confidence is high on the risk pattern, but lower than critical because no malicious payload is shown in the skill file.

medium_findings.title: Networked External Service Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run infsh apps for web search, X posting, image generation, and app listing. This can transmit prompts, draft content, or posting text to external services, so users should review inputs and credentials before running these commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly invoke networked infsh apps and include a posting example for X. This appears intentional for the skill, but it still creates privacy and publication risk if users run examples unchanged.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Matches in Markdown
low_findings.description: Several static detections are caused by Markdown code fences, inline backticks, or ordinary prose. The cited weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches do not show cryptographic implementation, credential handling, or host inspection logic in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found documentation text, examples, or content-writing guidance rather than executable Ruby backticks, weak crypto code, or system reconnaissance. The only real command risks are tracked separately.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Script Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start uses a curl-to-sh command. Users should prefer manual download, checksum verification, and review before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is explicit on line 14. The file also references checksum verification, but that does not remove the risk of piping remote script output directly to a shell.

content.user_title: Create LinkedIn Posts That Drive Engagement
content.value_statement: Professionals often know what they want to say but struggle to shape it for LinkedIn. This skill provides hooks, structure, formatting, and CTA patterns for clearer posts.
actual_capabilities: Drafts LinkedIn posts with hooks, body structure, and closing CTAs.
Applies mobile-friendly line breaks and short paragraph formatting.
Suggests post types such as personal stories, contrarian takes, carousels, polls, and lists.
Guides hashtag use, content pillars, and engagement-focused CTA choices.
Provides examples for optional visual generation through infsh apps.
limitations: Does not guarantee reach, impressions, comments, or follower growth.
LinkedIn algorithm guidance may become outdated and should be reviewed.
Networked infsh examples may send content to external services if executed.
Drafts still need human review for accuracy, tone, and brand policy.

use_cases.title: Founder Thought Leadership
use_cases.description: Turn product lessons, customer insights, or company updates into concise posts that invite discussion.

use_cases.title: Consultant Lead Generation
use_cases.description: Create educational posts that show expertise without relying on direct sales language.

use_cases.title: Career Storytelling
use_cases.description: Convert work experiences, failures, and lessons into posts that support a professional brand.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Simple Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single LinkedIn update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a LinkedIn post about [topic] for [audience]. Use a strong hook, short lines, one clear lesson, and a question CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite for LinkedIn
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this draft for LinkedIn. Improve the first 210 characters, remove jargon, add line breaks, and keep the message professional: [draft].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Content Pillar Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a post tied to a repeatable content strategy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a LinkedIn post for the [expertise/story/opinion/behind-the-scenes/curated insight] pillar. Audience: [audience]. Goal: [goal]. Include 3 hashtag suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Create Variants and Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for testing angles and engagement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three LinkedIn post variants from this idea: [idea]. Use different hook formulas, explain the best audience for each, and recommend the strongest CTA.

output_examples.input: A product manager wants a post about learning from a failed launch.
output_examples.output: A concise story-led post that opens with the failed launch, explains the missed assumption, shares three lessons, and ends by asking readers what they would validate first.

output_examples.input: A consultant wants to promote a new article without hurting reach.
output_examples.output: A standalone LinkedIn summary post with the main insight in the body, a soft note that the link is in comments, and a question that invites replies.

output_examples.input: An engineer wants to share a technical career lesson.
output_examples.output: A professional post with a pattern-interrupt hook, short mobile-friendly lines, a concrete example, and three relevant hashtags.

best_practices: Put the main reason to keep reading in the first two lines.
Use specific stories, data, or examples instead of broad claims.
Engage with comments after posting to support discussion momentum.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated claims without checking accuracy.
Do not paste confidential company, customer, or candidate details into external app commands.
Do not use generic corporate openings or excessive hashtags.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to LinkedIn?
faq.answer: No. It provides LinkedIn writing guidance and examples. Users should review and publish posts themselves.

faq.question: Can it create posts for company pages?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the company audience, topic, and brand tone so the draft matches the page.

faq.question: Does it support personal branding content?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes story, opinion, expertise, and behind-the-scenes formats for professional branding.

faq.question: Can it help with hooks?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists hook formulas and can rewrite openings for stronger first-line engagement.

faq.question: Are the algorithm tips guaranteed?
faq.answer: No. They are general guidance and may change as LinkedIn updates ranking behavior.

faq.question: Should I run the command examples?
faq.answer: Only run them after reviewing the destination services, inputs, credentials, and installation method.
